EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall amounts of 3 to 9 inches were measured across the area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Moisture increased over Utah beginning July 14, but the air near the surface remained dry, so gusty winds due to thunderstorms were more common than flash flooding on the 14th. On July 15, this moisture became deeper, and combined with a weather disturbance moving through the area to help produce several flash floods across the state of Utah. A single flash flood was reported on July 16.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Camel Back Mountain sensor in the Dugway Proving Ground mesonet recorded a wind gust of 59 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Severe thunderstorms continued to be a threat in northern Utah heading into the end of August, with multiple storms producing strong winds and large hail.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A maximum wind gust of 67 mph was recorded by the Simpson Springs sensor in the Dugway Proving Ground mesonet.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The month of July started out hot and dry.  However, by the middle of the month the Southwest Monsoon season had begun. The majority of the climate reporting stations reported near normal to slightly above normal monthly precipitation totals. Nonetheless, the drought worsened a bit in the far northeast corner of the state and remained in the severe category near the Four Corners.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Moderate drought conditions in the eastern Uinta Basin at the beginning of July deteriorated to severe to extreme drought conditions by the end of the month.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The month of July started out hot and dry.  However, by the middle of the month the Southwest Monsoon season had begun. The majority of the climate reporting stations reported near normal to slightly above normal monthly precipitation totals. Nonetheless, the drought worsened a bit in the far northeast corner of the state and remained in the severe category near the Four Corners.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Moderate drought conditions prevailed across most of the zone while severe drought conditions persisted in the Natural Bridges area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A very active monsoon thunderstorm outbreak caused flash floods and debris flows in southeast Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Significant amounts of small hail and rainfall led to flash flooding north of Monticello where 3 to 4 feet of water covered grassy areas near Highway 191. Flash flooding was reported from near the airport up to near Peters Hill which mainly impacted a lower road near the highway.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A very active monsoon thunderstorm outbreak caused flash floods and debris flows in southeast Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Numerous thunderstorms with heavy rainfall moved over the Arches National Park area. Washes ran fast and full, and several waterfalls were active in the park during the day. Wolfe Ranch Road and Delicate Arch Road were both closed by flood waters.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A moist westerly flow with embedded disturbances produced heavy snowfall mainly in the La Sal Mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall amounts of 12 to 17 inches were measured in the La Sal Mountains, with lesser amounts elsewhere within the zone.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A disturbance in a moist west to northwest flow aloft resulted in significant snowfall in the higher mountain areas of eastern Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall totals of 3 to 7 inches were measured across the area above the 8000 foot level.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A disturbance in a moist west to northwest flow aloft resulted in significant snowfall in the higher mountain areas of eastern Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall totals of 5 to 7 inches were measured across the area above the 8000 foot level.  Wind gusts of 25 to 40 mph produced areas of blowing and drifting snow.  Locally higher gusts on Abajo Peak were clocked up to 50 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A moist and moderate southwest flow ahead of an upper low produced significant to heavy snowfall and gusty winds in the mountains of eastern Utah.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This event which began on the last day of February and continued into March produced snowfall amounts of 5 to 8 inches across the area. Wind gusts of 25 to 45 mph produced extensive blowing and drifting snow.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: This event which began on the last day of February and continued into March produced snowfall amounts of 5 to 6 inches across the area. Wind gusts of 25 to 40 mph produced extensive blowing and drifting snow.
